Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (Canada-Barbados Income Tax Agreement)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Income Tax Act in order to include, in the definition âtaxable Canadian businessâ, any business that is entitled to a special tax benefit conferred by Barbados under the Canada-Barbados Income Tax Agreement Act, 1980.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The definition taxable Canadian business in subsection 95(1) of the Income Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: taxable Canadian business, at any time, of a foreign affiliate of a taxpayer resident in Canada or of a partnership of which a foreign affiliate of a taxpayer resident in Canada is a member (which foreign affiliate or partnership is referred to in this definition as the âoperatorâ), means a business

Subparagraph.Text: is, or would be if there were income from the business for the operatorâs taxation year or fiscal period that includes that time, included in computing the foreign affiliateâs taxable income earned in Canada for a taxation year under subparagraph 115(1)(a)(ii), and

is not, or would not be if there were income from the business for the operatorâs taxation year or fiscal period that includes that time, exempt, because of a tax treaty with a country, from tax under this Part, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is entitled to a special tax benefit conferred by Barbados under the Canada-Barbados Income Tax Agreement Act, 1980; (entreprise canadienne impoÂ­sable)
